title: Aureline settings sync scope-bundle and session-envelope
description: Boundary schema for the optional-sync scope bundle and the sync-session envelope that carries it. See docs/adr/0008-settings-definition-and-effective-configuration-resolver.md and docs/settings/sync_and_device_registry_seed.md. One scope-bundle payload carries a single-scope subset of setting-definition rows from a producer device to the resolver on another device; one sync-session envelope carries zero or more scope bundles plus the local-authoritative degrade posture. Raw secret bytes, trust grants, delegated credentials, machine-local paths, policy caches, and admin bundles never appear; the schema enumerates the omitted classes explicitly so new lanes cannot silently widen the contract. The eventual settings crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary that the settings UI, CLI settings sync command, profile import / export, support-bundle exporter, migration center, and optional-sync transport read. Adding a new top-level field, degrade posture, omitted class, or transport state is additive-minor and bumps settings_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

settings_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this sync scope-bundle / session-envelope vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. A sync_scope_bundle carries a single-scope subset of settings; a sync_session_envelope carries zero or more scope bundles plus the session-level degrade posture.

setting_id.description: Canonical stable dotted lower-snake setting id. MUST match a setting-definition row after alias redirection. The resolver denies entries whose id is unknown or retired.

syncable_scope_id.description: Frozen allow-list of scopes the optional-sync lane MAY carry by default. machine_specific, session_or_command_override, admin_policy_narrowing, workspace, folder_or_module_override, imported_profile_default, channel_or_experiment_default, and built_in_default are NEVER carried by the lane; the resolver refuses bundles that claim any other scope.

reason_class.description: Frozen reason class for bundle entries. Always 'sync' for this payload; the resolver mints a write_intent_packet with reason_class=sync when applying the entry on the pulling device.

actor_class.description: Frozen actor class of the producer-side write that put the value into the bundle. Mirrors the effective-setting record so the conflict packet and mutation journal share one vocabulary.

redaction_class.description: ADR-0007 redaction class applied to value previews inside the bundle. Entries whose declared redaction_class is 'exclude_from_export' MUST NOT appear in any bundle; the resolver drops them with a typed audit event.

value_preview_kind.description: How the value preview was produced for carriage in the bundle. 'excluded_from_export' is not reachable here because those entries are filtered out before the bundle is minted. 'credential_alias_only' carries the broker alias; never raw secret bytes.

value_preview.description: The preview payload, typed per the setting-definition value_type and redacted per the setting's redaction_class. Absent when value_preview_kind is absent.

class_label.description: Class label used when value_preview_kind is class_label_only. Never carries raw secret material.

credential_alias_ref.description: Broker alias handle copied in when value_preview_kind is credential_alias_only. Only the alias crosses the sync boundary; raw secret bytes never do.

value_preview_shape.description: One value-preview payload. Mirrors schemas/settings/write_intent.schema.json#/$defs/value_preview_shape but with the excluded_from_export variant forbidden.

widening_vector.description: Frozen widening-vector vocabulary. Mirrors schemas/settings/write_intent.schema.json#/$defs/widening_vector. Any non-'none' vector on a bundle entry MUST be accompanied by resolver-side denial with scope_broadening_would_widen_trust; a bundle that quietly widens trust is a bug.

would_widen_trust.description: True when applying this entry on the receiving device would silently widen workspace trust, AI egress, network egress, extension permissions, managed entitlement, credential exposure, or the declared allowed-scope set. A bundle carrying such an entry MUST be refused; the resolver emits a typed conflict packet with write_intent_verdict=denied.

narrowing_ceiling_intersected.description: True when the entry would be capped by an active admin-policy narrowing ceiling on the receiving device. Informational; never flips the verdict on its own but lets the 'Explain why' affordance render one trace.

note.description: Short human-readable note on the scope-broadening outcome. Redacted per ADR-0007.

scope_broadening_verdict.description: No-silent-scope-broadening verdict copied onto each bundle entry. Mirrors schemas/settings/write_intent.schema.json#/$defs/scope_broadening_verdict.

at.description: Monotonic stamp of the producer-side write that put the value into the bundle.

mutation_journal_ref.description: Opaque handle into the ADR-0006 mutation journal on the producer device for the write. Lets the receiving device quote the producer lineage without re-deriving it.

rollback_checkpoint_ref.description: Opaque handle into the ADR-0006 mutation journal on the producer device for the rollback checkpoint produced before the write. Present when the entry's preview_class required one; null otherwise.

last_written_block.description: Producer-side provenance for a bundle entry.

alias_redirected_from.description: Legacy id the producer-side write arrived on when a redirect fired; null if the producer wrote to the canonical id directly.

schema_version.description: The setting-definition schema version the producer wrote against. Null means the producer did not record a version; the receiver treats that as a stale payload and routes to local-authoritative.

migration_applied.description: from_version of the migration row the producer applied when normalising the value, if any. Null when no migration ran.

note.description: Short human-readable note. Redacted per ADR-0007; never carries raw secret material.

sync_scope_bundle_entry.description: One entry in a scope bundle. Carries exactly one setting at exactly one scope, along with the redaction class and the scope-broadening verdict the receiver evaluates before apply.

omitted_record_class.description: Frozen enumeration of record classes that the optional-sync lane MUST NOT carry. Every scope bundle explicitly lists these as omitted so a new lane cannot quietly widen the contract. The resolver refuses a bundle whose omitted_classes list does not fully cover this set.

local_authoritative_degrade_reason.description: Frozen degrade-reason vocabulary. Names why the session is running in local-authoritative mode. 'manual_export_import' is the user-initiated continuity path: export from one device, import on another; the envelope records it so support and audit can distinguish continuity from a live session.

session_state.description: Frozen session-state vocabulary. 'local_authoritative_degraded' is the default posture whenever any degrade_reason fires; the receiver keeps serving local values and NEVER replays stale, encrypted-failed, or capability-missing data silently. 'refused' is a terminal state the resolver sets when a bundle violates omitted_classes, widens trust, or targets a disallowed scope.

transport_state.description: Transport-level state. 'degraded' fires when the transport is up but round-trips are slow enough that the resolver has moved to local-authoritative until a fresh, signed payload arrives.

classes.description: List of omitted record classes. Every producer MUST include the full frozen set; the resolver refuses bundles missing any class as a safety measure against silent widening.

non_widening_affirmed.description: Producer-side affirmation that the bundle honours the non-widening rule (mirrors ADR 0008 Section 'Scope-broadening rule (frozen)'). MUST be true on every emitted bundle; a false or missing affirmation is refused with session_state=refused.

note.description: Short human-readable note on the omitted-classes block. Redacted per ADR-0007.

omitted_classes_block.description: Frozen omitted-classes block. The only path to declare what the bundle will NOT carry. Surfaces render this list verbatim; they do not mint parallel 'excluded' lists.

bundle_id.description: Opaque stable id for this scope bundle. Reused by the session envelope that carries it and by conflict packets the receiver emits while reconciling the bundle.

producer_device_id.description: Device id that produced this bundle. MUST match a sync_device_record whose revocation_state is active or paused; revoked and forgotten device ids are refused.

producer_lineage_cursor.description: Opaque monotonic cursor copied from the producer's device record at emit time. Lets the receiver order and dedupe bundles from the same device without re-deriving lineage.

reason_class.description: Reason class for the entries. Always 'sync' when present; null is equivalent.

entries.description: Zero or more setting entries carried in this bundle. Every entry shares the same producer_device_id and the same declared scope. An empty entries list is legal (for example, a heartbeat bundle establishing lineage without carrying a value).

emitted_at.description: Monotonic stamp the producer minted the bundle.

bundle_epoch.description: Monotonic per-device bundle epoch. The receiver refuses bundles whose epoch is not strictly greater than the last accepted epoch from the same device (stale-payload protection).

expires_at.description: When the bundle is no longer acceptable to the receiver; stale bundles route to local-authoritative. Null means no bound.

schema_version.description: The sync scope-bundle schema version. Distinct from settings_schema_version, which versions the envelope.

record_class_id.description: Stable handle into artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml naming the retention / export posture for the bundle when carried into a support bundle or offboarding packet.

policy_epoch.description: Policy epoch observed on the producer at emit time. Lets the receiver evaluate admin-policy narrowing against a stable baseline.

note.description: Short human-readable note. Redacted per ADR-0007.

sync_scope_bundle.description: One scope bundle. A single-scope subset of settings flowing between two devices through the optional-sync lane.

session_id.description: Opaque stable id for this sync session. Reused by conflict packets emitted inside the session, by audit events, and by the migration-center renderer.

local_device_id.description: Device id of the local (receiving / initiating) device. MUST match a sync_device_record.

remote_device_ids.description: Device ids of remote peers participating in this session. Empty when the session is local-authoritative with no live transport.

degrade_reasons.description: Active degrade reasons. Empty when session_state=open or reconciling. Every non-'open' session that is not 'refused' MUST list at least one reason; a degraded session with no named reason is a bug.

bundles.description: Zero or more scope bundles carried inside this session. Empty for pure session-control envelopes (for example, degrade announcements).

opened_at.description: Monotonic stamp the session was opened.

last_reconciled_at.description: Monotonic stamp the session most recently completed a reconciliation round. Null before the first reconciliation.

closed_at.description: Monotonic stamp the session was closed. Null while session_state is open, reconciling, local_authoritative_degraded, or paused.

carried_at.description: Monotonic stamp the user-initiated export was carried into the target device.

export_artifact_ref.description: Opaque handle pointing at the profile-export artifact that carried continuity. Never a raw filesystem path.

manual_continuity.description: Present when the session represents a manual export/import continuity path rather than a live transport. Null for live sessions.

schema_version.description: The sync session-envelope schema version. Distinct from settings_schema_version.

record_class_id.description: Stable handle into artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml naming the retention / export posture for the session envelope.

note.description: Short human-readable note. Redacted per ADR-0007.

sync_session_envelope.description: One sync-session envelope. Carries zero or more scope bundles plus the session-level degrade posture so receivers never have to infer local-authoritative state.
